title: Server actions and boundary validation
description: Expose Next.js server actions or route handlers that map external input to use case DTOs, validate all mutating inputs at the server boundary, invoke application use cases with injected repository/db factory, and translate domain and infrastructure failures into structured, user-safe error outcomes (invalid input, empty title, DB/migration failures) without leaking stack traces or internal paths in production responses.
acceptance: All mutating server entrypoints validate inputs before calling use cases.
Three explicit failure classes are observable by callers: invalid input, empty/forbidden title text, and DB/migration failures, each with safe user-facing messages.
Successful operations return data shapes the UI can render without exposing server file paths or secrets.
Wiring composes use cases with SQLite adapter using configuration from server-only env or resolved paths (not bundled to client).
No domain rules are reimplemented in actions beyond validation/mapping.
